content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIkMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
March 5,
1996
Honorable Laura Flores Espinosa
Mayor Pro Tern
City of Santa Paula
970 Ventura Street
Santa Paula,
CA
93061
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96--068
Dear Mayor Pro Tern Espinosa:
This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of
advice provided to you regarding your responsibilities under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place.
In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us.
The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Oglesby
(1975)
1 FPPC Ops.
71.)
QUESTION
May you participate in decisions affecting Jesse Ornelas,
or
the Nonprofit Economic Development Corporation,
of which
Mr.
Ornelas is a salaried employee?
CONCLUSION
According to your facts,
you do not have an economic interest
in Mr.
Ornelas or the Cabrillo Economic Development Corporation
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18995.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-96-068
Page.2
that would result in a conflict of interest.
If this is the case,
you may participate in the decisions concerning Mr.
Ornelas and
the corporation.
FACTS
You were elected to the Santa Paula City Council on
November 8,
1994.
Jesse Ornelas served as a volunteer co—
chairperson for your campaign committee.
Mr.
Ornelas was not
compensated,
nor did he make any contributions or gifts to you or
your conunittee.
The campaign concluded 15 months ago.
Mr.
Ornelas is a salaried employee of the nonprofit Cabrillo
Economic Development Corporation (the “corporation”)
.
The
corporation does business with the city on occasion.
For example,
in 1992
(before you were elected to the city council), the
corporation built a senior citizen complex.
More recently,
the
corporation requested a letter of support from the city council
for its application for special grant funds from the county.
The
corporation is also requesting a community development block grant
from the city of Santa Paula and it is anticipated that they will
request redevelopment funds from the city for their pending
project involving the building of
34 affordable townhomes in the
city.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official at any
level of state or local government from making, participating in
making or in any way attempting to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision which will have a reasonably
foreseeable and material financial effect on an economic interest.
Section 87103 provides that a public official has a financial
interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally,
on the official,
a member of his or her immediate
family,
or on any of the following:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public official has a
direct or indirect investment worth $1,000 or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public official has a
direct or indirect interest worth $1,000 or more.
(c)
Any source of income, other than gifts and other than
loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating $250 or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made.


Our File No. A96-068
Page
3
(d)
Any business entity in which the public official is a
director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any
position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or agent for a donor
of,
a gift or gifts aggregating $280 or more in value provided to,
received by,
or promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made.
2
According to your facts,
you do not have an economic interest
in either Mr.
Ornelas or the corporation.
Any involvement you had
with Mr.
Ornelas was more than 12 months ago and,
in any case,
did
not involve any payments to you either in the form of
contributions,
income or gifts.
Based on these facts,
you would
not have an economic interest in Mr. Ornelas or the corporation
and thus may participate in decisions affecting Mr.
Ornelas and
the corporation.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
Steven C.
Churchwell
General Counse
By:
ohn W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
2
The amount of the value of gifts specified by this subdivision
is adjusted biennially by the Commission to equal the gift limit
thresholds in Chapter 9.5 of the Act.
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of l0 per page.
